-module(solution).

%% solve(list(integer), integer) -> integer.
solve(Nums, K) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    if N == 0 ->
        -1;
    true ->
        PrefixMax = prefix_maxes(Nums),
        SuffixMin = suffix_mins(Nums),
        
        % Find the smallest stable index I
        find_stable_index(0, N, PrefixMax, SuffixMin, K)
    end.

%% Finds the smallest index I such that PMax[I] - SMin[I] <= K
find_stable_index(I, N, PMax, SMin, K) when I >= N ->
    -1; % No stable index found
find_stable_index(I, N, PMax, SMin, K) ->
    % Accessing elements by index in Erlang lists is O(I), but since we iterate sequentially
    % and N is the total length, the total time complexity remains O(N) overall.
    P_i = lists:nth(I, PMax),
    S_i = lists:nth(I, SMin),
    
    Instability = P_i - S_i,
    
    if Instability =< K ->
        I % Found the smallest stable index
    ;
        find_stable_index(I + 1, N, PMax, SMin, K) % Check next index
    end.

%% Calculates Prefix Maximums: [max(nums[0..0]), max(nums[0..1]), ..., max(nums[0..N-1])]
prefix_maxes([]) -> [];
prefix_maxes([H|T]) ->
    [H | prefix_maxes_recursive(T, H)].

prefix_maxes_recursive([], _Max) -> [];
prefix_maxes_recursive([H|T], CurrentMax) ->
    NewMax is max(CurrentMax, H),
    [NewMax | prefix_maxes_recursive(T, NewMax)].

%% Calculates Suffix Minimums: [min(nums[0..N-1]), ..., min(nums[N-1..N-1])]
suffix_mins(Nums) ->
    ReversedNums = lists:reverse(Nums),
    
    % We calculate prefix minimums on the reversed list.
    % The minimum of the suffix nums[i..N-1] corresponds to the minimum of 
    % the prefix [nums[N-1], ..., nums[i]] in the reversed list.
    
    % Start with the last element of the original list (head of ReversedNums)
    H = lists:hd(ReversedNums),
    T = lists:tail(ReversedNums),
    
    MinPrefixReversed = [H | prefix_mins_recursive_tail(T, H)],

    % Reverse back to get the suffix minimums array in correct order
    lists:reverse(MinPrefixReversed).

% Helper for calculating prefix minimums on a sequence already starting with the minimum element
prefix_mins_recursive_tail([], _Min) -> [];
prefix_mins_recursive_tail([H|T], CurrentMin) ->
    NewMin is min(CurrentMin, H),
    [NewMin | prefix_mins_recursive_tail(T, NewMin)].
```
